div1.entryFree: Chaos, the confused matter supposed to have existed before the creation: and, beauty dead, black c. comes again, Ven. 1020. vast sin-concealing c. (sc. the night), Lucr. 767. to disproportion me in every part, like to a c. or an unlicked bear-whelp, H6C III, 2, 161. this c., when degree is suffocate, Troil. I, 3, 125. mis-shapen c. of well-seeming forms, Rom. I, 1, 185. when I love thee not, c. is come again, Oth. III, 3, 92.
